content:
- Executed meta-task 18 pass-7 deep skeptical review with fresh evidence dir `.ralph/evidence/meta-18-pass7-20260304T205520Z`.
- Hardened Makefile gate bypass resistance: reject `TIMEOUT_BIN` when set via environment; refuse `MAKEFLAGS=-e`.
- Removed all `#[allow(dead_code)]` suppressions in `src/test_harness/provenance.rs` and added lightweight policy/attestation metadata validation.
- Fixed contributor-doc drift (etcd harness readiness wording; codebase-map now matches required chapter shape) and updated Node API docs (fallback endpoints; debug/verbose optional since).
- Made verification report reachable in SUMMARY and added historical-scope notice to avoid stale-path misleadingness; deleted obsolete contributor stub.
- Fixed flake in `dcs::etcd_store::tests::etcd_store_disconnect_clears_pending_queue_before_reconnect_snapshot` (allow early Reset marker while still banning stale queued events).
- Ran required gates and archived logs: `make check`, `make test` (after-fix), `make lint`, `make test-long` all pass.
- Created/closed bug tasks from subagents: docs drift + provenance helper compile break.
- Set `.ralph/model.txt` back to `normal_high` and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`.

- Next: stage + commit + push; then stop. No further tasks queued for this pass.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and scanned for any <passes>false</passes>/<passing>false</passing> entries.
- Confirmed all bug tasks are currently marked passing; found several non-bug tasks still non-passing.
- Selected next task as the next unstarted step in the pgBackRest managed backup/recovery story: story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/02 (managed archive/recovery takeover before recovery starts).
- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next: switch to implementation agent for that task and run make check/test/lint after completion.

- Subagent task: inspected archive/logging pipeline for explicit archive ownership + restore/archive structured events.
- Read src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs and src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs plus process worker/runtime/config wiring.
- Findings: archive wrapper is only generated by ingest worker and not owned by startup/config orchestration; wrapper emits JSON lines with nested pgtuskmaster.archive but ingest parser currently keeps whole JSON under postgres.json without flattening stable attrs; wrapper setup errors are swallowed in ingest run(); python3 hard dependency in wrapper can break archive_command.
- Process worker already emits structured pg tool lines with stable job_id/job_kind/binary attrs for pgbackrest_restore/archive_push/archive_get. Proposed integrating archive+restore ownership under process worker and adding normalized event schema with operation/status/stanza/repo/wal fields.
- Next (for parent agent): implement explicit archive command manager + restore command manager in managed config/startup flow, add stable attribute extraction in postgres_ingest for archive wrapper JSON and pgbackrest output, and add tests for schema stability + failure paths.

- Inspected process job model (`src/process/jobs.rs`, `src/process/state.rs`, `src/process/worker.rs`) plus pgBackRest builders (`src/backup/worker.rs`, `src/backup/pgbackrest.rs`) and archive wrapper logging path.
- Confirmed pgBackRest restore/archive command kinds already exist and build CLI args, but are stringly-typed (`Vec<String>` options) with duplicate validation and minimal structured log context.
- Next: hand back a concrete refactor proposal for typed pgBackRest specs/options, stricter validation, and richer structured output capture tied to command context.

- Investigated startup orchestration in src/runtime/node.rs in detail with line references.
- Confirmed startup modes are InitializePrimary, CloneReplica, ResumeExisting; no restore-aware mode exists.
- Confirmed pgBackRest restore/archive job kinds exist in process layer (jobs/state/worker) but are not wired into runtime startup execution.
- Confirmed managed config materialization currently happens only in run_start_job right before StartPostgres, and only emits hba/ident/tls overrides via -c options; no explicit recovery.signal/standby.signal/restore_command orchestration exists.
- Confirmed archive wrapper creation currently runs in postgres_ingest worker after startup, so it is not guaranteed to exist before first recovery boot.
- Next: provide parent a concrete restore-aware startup plan with insertion points in runtime/node.rs and supporting modules (process/jobs, backup worker, postgres_managed, logging bootstrap ordering).

- scanned current coverage for recovery/pgBackRest/startup across src/runtime/node.rs, src/postgres_managed.rs, src/backup/*, src/process/*, src/logging/* and docs/src/operator/*.
- confirmed startup mode tests are only selection-level; no startup execution ordering tests for restore/takeover-before-recovery; pgBackRest tests are mostly command rendering/building plus one real version job; archive wrapper has real ingest coverage.
- next: hand back concrete proposed unit/integration test matrix and operator docs pages to update (configuration/troubleshooting/observability/deployment/index + optional new recovery runbook page).

- Traced managed config + startup + process + logging paths for task 02 (archive/recovery takeover).
- Confirmed `materialize_managed_postgres_config` only owns hba/ident/tls + `-c` settings; no pre-recovery takeover of restored config artifacts.
- Confirmed runtime startup only has InitializePrimary/CloneReplica/ResumeExisting; no restore startup mode. `PgBackRestRestore`/archive job specs exist but are only validated/built, not dispatched by runtime.
- Confirmed archive wrapper is ad-hoc cp script created by postgres_ingest worker after startup begins, not as startup-owned archive/recovery control.
- Prepared concrete injection points and file-by-file change+test plan: add restore startup mode, takeover helper in postgres_managed, move archive control into startup-managed config, and add restore-focused unit/e2e/logging tests.

- Inspected repository hook wiring and found `.githooks/post-commit` already executing `scripts/publish-docs.sh` after every commit.
- Implemented macOS bypass in `.githooks/post-commit` by adding an early `uname -s == Darwin` check that exits 0 immediately.
- Hook now prints `[post-commit] skipped on macOS` and does nothing on mac; non-mac behavior unchanged.
- No other files changed.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and opened task 02 managed archive/recovery bootstrap takeover.
- Inspected current code paths: `src/runtime/node.rs` startup modes/ordering, `src/postgres_managed.rs` managed config, pgBackRest job/spec plumbing, and current archive wrapper + ingest behavior.
- Spawned explorer subagents to review runtime startup, managed config takeover seams, process job model, logging wrapper/ingest, and tests/docs coverage.
- Wrote a full detailed implementation plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/02-task-managed-archive-recovery-bootstrap-config-takeover.md` and appended `TO BE VERIFIED` as instructed.
- Next: reopen the task file, do a deep skeptical verification pass (must change at least one plan item), then replace `TO BE VERIFIED` with `NOW EXECUTE` before starting implementation.

- Inspected process job model + pgBackRest integration flow across src/process/jobs.rs, src/process/state.rs, src/process/worker.rs, src/backup/pgbackrest.rs, src/backup/worker.rs.
- Traced command construction path: config -> backup::worker job constructors -> ProcessJobKind specs (with Vec<String> options) -> process::worker::build_command -> backup::pgbackrest::render -> ProcessCommandRunner spawn.
- Confirmed duplicate validation in config parser and backup renderer; both use string-prefix checks for stanza/repo override prevention.
- Noted stringly-typed operation dispatch in backup::worker ("backup"/"info"/...) and repetitive pgbackrest branches in process::worker build_command/timeout/labels.
- Found validation asymmetry and bypass risk surface from raw option tokens (forbidden override coverage is partial and currently expressed as prefix-based checks), plus low direct coverage for backup::worker and limited negative tests for pgBackRest validation.
- Next (if asked to implement): introduce typed pgBackRest operation/options model, centralize option validation+rendering once, replace op string dispatch with enum, tighten forbid-override policy by construction, and add focused unit/integration tests (including spy runner capture for start_job path and config->job->args mapping tests).

- Reviewed managed Postgres takeover + recovery handling focused on src/postgres_managed.rs, src/runtime/node.rs startup path, src/ha/decide.rs + src/ha/worker.rs dispatch, and process command building.
- Mapped current behavior against plan notes in RUST_SYSTEM_HARNESS_PLAN.md (especially future TODO section around owning archive/recovery and conf takeover).
- Key findings: FollowLeader/SignalFailSafe are currently no-op dispatches; no explicit recovery.signal/standby.signal takeover path; startup clone chooses leader id but still uses static rewind_source host/port; resume-existing with postmaster.pid bypasses managed config materialization; action dedupe via recent_action_ids is permanent and can suppress retries; recovery phase transitions key off generic last_outcome without job-kind correlation.
- Verified unwrap/expect/panic patterns in focal files using rg and found no matches.
- Next immediate step if continuing: propose concrete plan deltas with explicit new pipeline stages (managed takeover phase, recovery signal materialization, follow-leader side effects, retry/idempotency redesign with operation-scoped keys, and job-kind-aware process outcomes) and then optionally implement highest-risk fixes first (retry suppression + follow leader side effects).

- Inspected logging archive/ingest pipeline deeply: src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs, src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs, src/logging/tailer.rs, process worker logging, runtime startup wiring, managed postgres config.
- Mapped current behavior: archive wrapper generation currently lives in ingest worker startup path and errors are ignored there; ingest step errors and cleanup errors are also dropped silently.
- Confirmed wrapper still uses cp + python3 JSON emission; restore_command path not implemented; startup code does not yet own archive_command/restore_command in managed config.
- Identified concrete risk cluster: cleanup only protects pg_ctl file and can delete active log_dir files; no path ownership validation for sink/source overlap can cause self-ingest loops; dir tailers never prune removed files.
- Opened and reviewed task plan .ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/02-task-managed-archive-recovery-bootstrap-config-takeover.md and compared feasibility against current code.
- Created grouped bug task .ralph/tasks/bugs/logging-archive-ingest-silent-failure-and-unsafe-cleanup.md for silent failure + cleanup/path overlap issues.
- Next if continuing: implement plan updates first (path ownership validation + startup-owned wrapper generation + typed archive/restore event schema), then patch code in small verified steps with tests.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and opened task 02: .ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/02-task-managed-archive-recovery-bootstrap-config-takeover.md (was at TO BE VERIFIED).
- Spawned 3 explorer subagents to do deep skeptical verification across postgres_managed/runtime startup, logging archive wrapper/ingest, and pgBackRest process job model.
- Key skeptical findings incorporated into plan:
  - Recovery signal files must be owned (don’t inherit backup-era recovery.signal/standby.signal); restore bootstrap should create a fresh recovery.signal and quarantine existing signals.
  - Wrapper JSON escaping in shell is fragile; prefer pgtuskmaster-owned compiled helper/subcommand; also require concurrency-safe JSONL appends.
  - Add config validation invariants to prevent logging sink/source overlap and accidental cleanup/tail loops.
  - Refine pgBackRest option override forbids to use exact keys (allow --repo1-* etc); avoid prefix-based false positives.
  - Added test bullets for wrapper path escaping + concurrency and config path invariants.
- Updated the task plan accordingly and replaced the end marker with NOW EXECUTE.
- Noted new bug task file exists: .ralph/tasks/bugs/logging-archive-ingest-silent-failure-and-unsafe-cleanup.md (from subagent review).

- Next: follow the NOW EXECUTE plan exactly (no more exploration), implement restore-bootstrap startup mode + managed takeover + pgBackRest pg1-path ownership + wrapper/ingest refactor, then run make check/test/test-long/lint until 100% green.

- New user request: use subagents + internet to read relevant PostgreSQL 16 docs and explain exactly how backups work.
- Read prior progress log successfully; now launching multiple explore_spark agents in parallel with narrowly scoped doc sections and source-link requirements.
- Will synthesize a single accurate backup model (logical dump vs physical base backup + WAL archiving/PITR + restore flows + tooling and gotchas) with citations.

- Gathered PostgreSQL 16 official documentation for file-system-level physical backups, snapshots, and WAL/restore requirements.
- Queried official docs sections 26.2 (File-system-level backups) and 26.3 (Continuous archiving and backup). Extracted consistency constraints, snapshot caveats, offline-vs-online restore implications, and required PostgreSQL settings.
- Next: respond to user with a compact, source-cited summary of offline backups, snapshot behavior, WAL requirements, and restore implications using official PostgreSQL documentation URLs.

- Searched official PostgreSQL 16 documentation for pg_basebackup and pg_verifybackup via web sources.
- Confirmed key physical backup behavior: plain vs tar formats, WAL inclusion modes, manifest generation/controls, and verification options.
- Collected exact option details from primary docs to answer format, WAL streaming, and integrity verification requirements.
- Next: provide user-facing summary with cited docs links and key flags for operational guidance.

- User asked for PostgreSQL 16 recovery docs with concrete semantics for restore_command, recovery.signal, recovery targets, and timelines.
- Ran web research against official PostgreSQL 16 documentation pages: runtime-config-wal, continuous-archiving, and recovery-config.
- Extracted key semantics:
  - recovery target mode started via recovery.signal; standby.signal takes precedence.
  - restore_command expansion/exit semantics and nonzero-for-missing behavior.
  - recovery_target exclusivity rules and behavior for immediate/name/time/xid/lsn/inclusive.
  - recovery_target_timeline defaults/values (latest/current/explicit numeric or hex) and usage constraints.
  - recovery.target action pause/promote/shutdown, including shutdown caveat and pause semantics.
  - timeline-history behavior and recovery default to latest timeline.
  - recovery.conf migration removal in PG12+ and prohibition of recovery.conf file.
- No code changes made in this response; no tests run.

- User asked to use btca to add postgres16 resource.
- Followed btca-cli skill: checked current resources, checked btca add help, added resource with `btca add -n postgres16 -b REL_16_STABLE https://github.com/postgres/postgres`.
- Verified with `btca resources` that `postgres16` now exists and points to REL_16_STABLE.

- Pulled official PostgreSQL 16 docs for continuous archiving, pg_basebackup, and recovery settings.
- Consolidated artifact/retention/WAL replay model and critical pitfalls into a source-backed operational summary.
- No repo code changes made in this step; response prepared from docs only.

- Task: user requested official PostgreSQL 16 backup runbook from docs.
- Ran web search/open on PostgreSQL 16 docs and extracted official sections: backup chapter, backup-dump, continuous-archiving, app-pgbasebackup, app-pgverifybackup, app-pg-dumpall, app-pgrestore, runtime-config-wal.
- Captured required operational detail for 4 sections: logical backup, physical base backup, PITR recovery, and validation.
- Next: provide strict runbook with only official postgresql.org URLs.

- Attempted to use btca postgres16 and hit repeated git clone failures from the configured remote resource.
- Diagnosed that btca resource cache was stale/failed and resolved by cloning postgres16 locally, re-registering `postgres16` as a local btca resource, then rerunning the query.
- Retrieved PostgreSQL 16 docs/source-backed explanation covering pg_hba.conf `hostssl`/`hostnossl`, `clientcert`, `clientname`, CN vs DN identity mapping, and auth-method interactions with `cert`, `scram-sha-256`, and `md5`.
- Key follow-up: craft response with exact matching/precedence semantics and caveats (method-independent `clientcert`, method-specific identity checks, mapping via `pg_ident.conf`, and CA chain/sslmode dependencies).

- Completed user request using subagents + internet: spawned large explore_spark batch over PG16 backup docs sections and additional 3-agent end-to-end cross-check set.
- Aggregated and reconciled results across logical dumps, physical base backups, continuous archiving/PITR, recovery signal semantics, manifests/verification, performance/large cluster notes, and PG16 change notes.
- Confirmed current PG16 minor from official release pages is 16.13 (release date 2026-02-26).
- Next if context continued: provide environment-specific runbook (exact commands/config) tailored to user infra and desired RPO/RTO.

- Read prior context and rehydrated local btca `postgres16` source mirror at `/tmp/postgres16_btca_repo` after Git fetch via btca failed initially.
- Queried btca only with `-r postgres16` for TLS docs and source behavior: `sslmode`, hostname verification, client cert/key/CA/CRL options, and `channel_binding` with SCRAM.
- Confirmed client-side SSL options present in PG16 libpq and identified several requested names as unsupported (`sslpassfile`, `sslidentity`, `sslkeypassword`, `sslrootcertdir`, `sslca`) in this resource snapshot.
- Prepared a complete model summary: SSL negotiation precedence per `sslmode`, certificate + hostname matching rules, CRL handling, and channel-binding failure conditions.

- User asked to use explore_spark and explain TLS auth in PG16.
- Spawned 3 explore_spark subagents with distinct scopes: server TLS handshake/defaults, pg_hba client cert semantics, and libpq sslmode/channel binding behavior.
- Collected and compared outputs; consistent model: TLS encryption/identity and database auth are separate layers combined via hostssl + clientcert/cert + auth method.
- Next response will provide concise end-to-end explanation and practical secure baseline config guidance.

- Executed direct file-read proof request against postgres16 resource.
- Read `/tmp/postgres16_btca_repo/src/backend/postmaster/postmaster.c` and `/tmp/postgres16_btca_repo/src/include/libpq/pqcomm.h` directly.
- Confirmed line counts and extracted exact `NEGOTIATE_SSL_CODE` matches plus surrounding lines for context.
- No `SSLRequest` token occurred in these two files; used matching `NEGOTIATE_SSL_CODE` lines as requested.

- Read required PostgreSQL 16 source files directly from `/tmp/postgres16_btca_repo/src/interfaces/libpq`:
  - `fe-connect.c`
  - `fe-secure-openssl.c`
- Extracted direct evidence via line-numbered reads using `awk`:
  - `fe-connect.c` line count is 7827; first `sslmode`-containing line is at 274.
  - `fe-connect.c` first `SSL_`-pattern line is at 1612.
  - `fe-secure-openssl.c` line count is 2087; first `verify-full`-containing line is at 1038.
  - `fe-secure-openssl.c` first `SSL_`-pattern line is at 63.
- No file edits were made.

- Task: direct file-read proof for postgres16 resource targets.
- Confirmed requested files are not present in repo root; located them under `/tmp/postgres16_btca_repo`.
- Read `/tmp/postgres16_btca_repo/doc/src/sgml/libpq.sgml` and `/tmp/postgres16_btca_repo/src/backend/libpq/hba.c` once each.
- Extracted authoritative evidence: total line counts, first non-empty line, and exact pattern match line for each (`sslmode`, `clientcert`, `hostssl`).
- Will report exact absolute path, counts, and raw matching lines to user.

- User asked to have explore_spark prove direct read access to pg16 repo.
- Spawned 3 explore_spark agents with explicit file-read proof requirements (absolute paths, line counts, matching lines with numbers).
- Received concrete evidence across 6 files under /tmp/postgres16_btca_repo.
- Performed independent local spot-check via wc -l and rg; reported line counts and key lines match subagent outputs.

- User asked to check docker creation tasks for explicit checks that setup works when only Docker is installed.
- Read progress log, searched .ralph/tasks and current_tasks for docker/container task definitions.
- Verified only active docker creation task is .ralph/tasks/story-container-first-deployment/01-task-container-first-docker-deployment-and-compose.md.
- Findings: task implies container-first/no ad-hoc local binary setup and requires compose config + endpoint health checks, but does not include an explicit acceptance check that host has only Docker/Compose installed (clean-host prerequisite gate).
- Next: report gap clearly and offer to patch acceptance criteria with explicit “fresh host with only Docker/Compose” verification steps.

- Performed B2 log-density cross-check for `src`.
- Verified no `trace!`, `debug!`, `info!`, `warn!`, or `error!` macro invocations exist in any file under `src`.
- Confirmed no namespaced variants (e.g., `tracing::info!`, `log::error!`) exist either.
- Confirmed `Cargo.toml` has no direct logging crate dependency (`tracing`/`log` absent), though transitive crates still pull `log/tracing`.
- Since no matching macro calls, all modules are effectively 0/0 for the requested macro set; no per-event logging inside loops can exist.
- Only direct event-style output macros found are `println!/eprintln!` in `src/bin/pgtuskmasterctl.rs` (11, 15) and `src/bin/pgtuskmaster.rs` (19), outside of loops.

- Inspected logging initialization and runtime wiring in `src/runtime/node.rs` + `src/logging/*` + config parser/schema/defaults.
- Confirmed startup flow: load config -> validate runtime config -> bootstrap logging once -> pass immutable `LogHandle` into workers.
- Tracked sinks/formatting: stderr and optional file JSONL sinks, fanout behavior, severity filtering, and Postgres ingest parsing/normalization path.
- Tracked retention/rotation behavior: per-file-tail inode-based rotation for tailing, explicit app-log cleanup (max files + age) for postgres log_dir, no built-in rotation for logging sink beyond append/truncate mode.
- Confirmed no runtime API/state mechanism to change `logging.level` after startup; no config reload path for `LogHandle` observed.
- Identified likely defects: startup-only log level, non-self-rotating app sinks, and minimal hostname derivation (`HOSTNAME` env only).

- User asked: explore how logging works and whether logging is frequent.
- Read prior progress memory, ran local scans (`rg`, `nl`) and spawned 4 explore_spark subagents with overlapping scopes (architecture + frequency) to cross-check claims.
- Confirmed logging is a custom structured JSONL pipeline (not tracing/log macros): runtime bootstraps logging once in `run_node_from_config`, then shared `LogHandle` is passed to workers.
- Confirmed sinks: stderr and optional file sink with append/truncate only; fallback NullSink when both disabled.
- Confirmed high-volume paths are process child-output drain loop (10ms poll) and postgres log tail ingest loop (poll interval default 200ms, emits per tailed line).
- Confirmed no `trace!/debug!/info!/warn!/error!` macro callsites in src; application logs come from explicit `log.emit(...)`/`emit_record(...)` and ingestion paths.
- Noted practical caveat: dynamic runtime log-level changes are not wired; threshold is fixed at bootstrap.
- Next if continuing: offer to produce a quick quantitative instrumentation patch (counter of emitted records per producer/severity) to answer “how often” empirically during a real run.

- Follow-up user concern: under-logging and operator-debuggability across workers/actions.
- Audited api/ha/dcs/pginfo/process/debug_api paths with line-level refs.
- Found concrete under-logging patterns: API worker swallows step_once errors, postgres_ingest swallows step and cleanup errors, process worker drops emit_subprocess_line errors, HA dispatch records faults in state but lacks explicit action-attempt/result event logs.
- Filed grouped bug: .ralph/tasks/bugs/logging-observability-under-logging-and-silent-worker-errors.md per repo instruction.
- Will respond with operator-focused assessment: what is observable now (debug snapshots, state transitions, child output ingestion) vs blind spots and practical debug workflow.

- User requested to alter the bug task after clarifying parse-failure expectations.
- Updated bug file to explicitly preserve parse-failure fallback semantics (must keep original raw content, no line drop on parse failure).
- Narrowed intent to true silent-loss paths (swallowed worker errors and suppressed emit failures) and added acceptance criteria for both preservation and silent-swallow removal/surfacing.

- User requested use add-task-as-agent skill: create a general new logging task and remove prior bug task.
- Added new task under story-rust-system-harness: 42-task-operator-grade-action-logging-and-no-silent-errors.md.
- Task encodes requested severity policy: debug for all actions, info for operator-important defaults/lifecycle, warn for ignorable errors, error for hard failures.
- Task explicitly requires same existing logging infra/interface everywhere and includes verification criterion that runtime-path errors are not silently swallowed.
- Preserved parse-fallback requirement in acceptance criteria to avoid regressions.
- Deleted bug file: .ralph/tasks/bugs/logging-observability-under-logging-and-silent-worker-errors.md as requested.

- User requested task clarity: not just actions, but pervasive debug logging throughout code so runtime can be traced exactly.
- Updated task 42 description/scope/acceptance to require broad debug breadcrumbs across worker loops, branch decisions, IO boundaries, orchestration transitions, and module/function origins with correlation ids.
- Expanded acceptance criteria coverage modules to include dcs/pginfo/api/process/runtime/ha/logging ingest for both breadcrumb presence and no swallowed errors.

- User asked why archive wrapper uses bash and called it a mess.
- Investigated implementation path: src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs + src/postgres_managed.rs + docs/src/operator/observability.md + src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs.
- Confirmed design intent: generated shell wrapper is injected into postgres archive_command/restore_command so pgtuskmaster can capture per-invocation JSON records and keep original exit code semantics.
- Confirmed why shell exists today: postgres archive_command/restore_command are command strings executed by shell; wrapper gives one stable executable path with embedded cfg and logging behavior.
- Noted real mess points to call out candidly: string-generated script, handcrafted JSON escaping, date/sed/tr/truncation pipelines, and log append atomicity assumptions; also ingestion worker currently ignores step errors in main loop (separate bug already known in progress history).
- Next step in reply: give direct explanation + practical cleanup direction (replace generated shell with compiled rust wrapper binary/subcommand and structured JSON writer while preserving postgres command semantics).

- User asked whether new tasks should be moved to a different story.
- Confirmed tasks 43/44 were initially created under story-rust-system-harness.
- Moved them to story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery and renumbered to 07/08 for local sequence consistency.
- Updated old 02 task superseded_by tags and superseded note to point to new story/task ids.
- Tightened task 08 language earlier to require generic argv-only passthrough, absolute-path-only binaries, no PATH lookup, including pgbackrest.

- User requested prioritization order change: cleanup/rebuild tasks must be immediately after task 02 in pgbackrest story.
- Renumbered story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery tasks so new cleanup/rebuild are now 03/04.
- Shifted previous 03-06 down to 05-08 to preserve sequence and priority intent.
- Updated in-file cross-task references impacted by renumbering (task 01 refs old 04->06; task 02 refs old 03->05).
- Updated superseded_by links and superseded note in story-rust-system-harness/02 to point to new 03/04 ids in pgbackrest story.
